items.name: Energy
items.description:
Must have at least 1 unit for fighting. A unit is 100 Kilojoules.

After each fight, consume 1 energy with the recipe that shows a trash can. Restore by levelling up, or travel to the *Maintenance Building* and click on the *Recharging Station*.

Limit is 5 + 2 * [XL](/play/attrib/4/subject/13), plus any special bonuses. Record the bonuses by editing this description:

- No special bonuses yet

items.name: Pool of Water
items.description: Can be used to extinguish flames.

items.name: Recharging Station
items.description: Click on *Energy* to power up the robot.

locations.name: Baby Mice Area
locations.description: The weakest of the rodents can be found here, but the area is alarmingly close to the base. Populations must be controlled so they don't spread further.

locations.name: Highway
locations.description: Advanced state of disrepair, though it beats trudging through swampland. Provides travel deeper south, for a higher degree of difficulty than the areas near the Maintenance Building.

locations.name: Maintenance Building
locations.description: An outpost in northern Florida set up to contain the infestation. Engineers perform upgrades and repairs as well as charging. This is also where robots are controlled from, and where intel is assessed.

characters.name: 🐁Baby Mouse
characters.description: Mutant, rabid and green-eyed, burning from within due to toxic waste. Max 5 HP.

characters.name: 🐰Bad Hare Day
characters.description:
Red-eyed monster that never stops hopping up and down, up and down.

- Set difficulty to *Hard*.
- On victory, mark the boss as [Defeated](/play/attrib/3/char/5) and add **250 XP**.

characters.name: 🐭Full-grown Mouse
characters.description: Okay, it's still kind of cute, despite the toxic green eyes.

characters.name: 🤖 Herbert 2100B
characters.description:
Remotely controlled machine — the player character.

Does not get diseases from being bitten, but has a limited amount of power. Battle testing allows the engineers to improve the technology incrementally.

characters.name: 👴Old Fart Supreme
characters.description: Wearing a nametag with "Gerry" written on it.

characters.name: 🐇Tainted Bunny
characters.description: Fur is greenish and sticky, with a fuzzy white tail.

attribs.name: Accuracy
attribs.description: Or use Action Speed instead, meaning a guaranteed hit if we can land a hit on the target fast enough? Because robots should be 100% accurate unless something is damaged.

attribs.name: Action Speed
attribs.description: How fast to take an action.

attribs.name: Boost
attribs.description: Set to the result of [Surge](/play/event/44) for 1 round. It goes away even if the enemy doesn't choose to attack in that round.

attribs.name: Defeated
attribs.description: An enemy with this attribute is unique, so after victory, set to true.

attribs.name: Distance
attribs.description: Measure of how far away the enemy is (Theater of the Mind, because this scenario does not use a grid). Melee distance is 1.

attribs.name: Force
attribs.description: How much power is available to hit or move things. Equates to Strength for non-robots (or we could use a separate Str attrib).

attribs.name: HP
attribs.description: How much damage the enemy can take before being defeated. 0 HP doesn't necessarily mean dead — see the Finish Fight event for what happens at that point.

attribs.name: Movement Speed
attribs.description: How fast to reposition in the room.

attribs.name: Voltage
attribs.description: Determines electrical damage of an attack as well as how much energy is consumed.

attribs.name: XL
attribs.description:
Gaining a new level increases [energy limit](/configure/item/5) and sets [current energy](/play/item/5?owner_id=13) to full. On reaching level 3, see [Upgrade Bonus](/play/event/23?subject_id=13). 
Level  Total XP Required
~~~~~  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1      (0)
2      5
3      15
4      30
5      50
6      80
7      120
8      180
9      260
10     370
11     500
12     660
13     860
14     1110
15     1400
16     1800
17     2300
18     2900
19     3600
20     4200

attribs.name: XP
attribs.description:
Dispatch rodents to gain experience — bigger ones yield more.

Also, check whether it's time to increase [XL](/play/attrib/2/char/1).

events.name: ATK Bite (To-Hit)
events.description:
Use teeth for a basic melee attack.

- **Difficulty:** If you're significantly outleveled, for example level 1 or 2 to the enemy's 5, then set difficulty to *Easy*. If you significantly outlevel the enemy, then set difficulty to *Hard*.
- **Damage:** If the attack hits then [Roll for Damage](/play/event/40?subject_id=${subject_id}).
- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) Enemy gets two damage rolls instead of one.
- **Major Failure:** Enemy loses a turn.

events.name: ATK Bite Dmg
events.description: Roll, then decrease Robot HP by that amount.

events.name: ATK Fist Pummel (To-Hit)
events.description:
Close grasping digits and swing robotic arm forward. Basic melee attack.

- **Energy**: The robot must have at least 1 to start. After the attack, consume 1 [Energy](/play/item/5?owner_id=13).
- **Difficulty:** If you're significantly outleveled, for example level 1 or 2 to the enemy's 5, then set difficulty to *Hard*. If you significantly outlevel the opponent, then set difficulty to *Easy*.
- **Damage:** If the attack hits then [Roll for Damage](/play/event/38?subject_id=13).
- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) Roll two damage rolls instead of one.
- **Major Failure:** Consume 2 Energy instead of 1.

After the swing, the enemy gets to [take an action](/play/event/41) if they're still alive.

events.name: ATK Fist Pummel Dmg
events.description:
- Roll, then decrease Enemy HP by that amount.
- If Enemy HP is reduced to 0 then [Finish Fight](/play/event/18?subject_id=13).

Do we need a separate dmg roll? Ideally the hit roll would do everything. We could use determinate damage based on force and enemy resistance.

events.name: ATK Ignite
events.description:
Burning enemies take damage each round. Typically lasts 3 rounds. Damage per round is decided by the original event outcome.

- **Energy**: Make sure the robot has at least 1 to start.
- **Difficulty:** If you're significantly outleveled, for example level 1 or 2 to the enemy's 5, then set difficulty to *Hard*. If you significantly outlevel the opponent, then set difficulty to *Easy*, although that is probably a good time to move on to the next location.

Roll, then see [Finish Fight](/play/event/18?subject_id=13).

events.name: Choose Enemy Action
events.description: Roll to determine what enemy does next. If there is a straightforward choice in the narrative then there is no need to roll.

events.name: DEF Dodge
events.description: Attempt to evade a hit entirely or partially.

events.name: DEF Shock
events.description: Attempt to resist getting shocked. Is this necessary or can the attack event do the necessary roll?

events.name: Finish Fight
events.description:
If the fight ended in victory, then add the enemy's XL to [XP](/play/attrib/2/subject/13). To do this, press *Resolve*. Once the outcome is set, fill for XP and apply.

To fight the same kind of enemy again (not bosses which are unique), reset enemy stats.

events.name: Move to Range
events.description:
Step out of melee range, repositioning to about 5 tiles away.

- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) No loss of turn—the action is free.
- **Major Failure:** Allow an *Attack of Opportunity*.

events.name: Surge
events.description:
Gain a bonus to power and speed for 1 turn.

- Normally costs a turn to use. However, if you had just withdrawn to range and the enemy used their turn to follow, then it does not.
- Can only be used once per fight.
- Chance of causing a penalty rather than a bonus.

events.name: Upgrade Bonus
events.selection_strings:
Triple XP for the next three fights.
Gain 50% of XP towards the next level.
Increase Energy limit by 1.
For three fights, do not decrease Energy unless critical.
No Bonus.

overall settings.title: Infestation
overall settings.description:
After a disastrous lab experiment, Florida has been overrun with mutant rodents. Especially dangerous ones are further south. Vacationers and residents have fled. Many retired folks are missing.

Hack 'n slash dueling where you {lime|level up}.
